t_ch: 想和缇宝聊聊天！
t_en: I want to chat with Tribbie!

t_ch: 当然可以！小灰想聊些什么呢？又想听*我们*给你唱预言的歌谣吗？
t_en: No problem! What would you like to discuss, Little Gray? Do you want to hear our song of prophecy again?

t_ch: 我想…听听你们旅行的故事。
t_en: I want to... hear stories of your travels.

t_ch: 当然可以啦！*我们*在世间旅行千年，每个人都有丰富有趣的经历呢！小灰具体想听什么故事呀？
t_en: Of course! We've traveled the world for thousands of years — each of us has countless rich and fascinating experiences. What kind of story would you like to hear, Little Gray?

t_ch: 想听听缇宝经历的故事。
t_en: I want to hear about Tribbie's experiences.

t_ch: *我们*吗？*我们*记得自己离开雅努萨波利斯的时候，手里还拿着家乡的纺锤。
t_en: We? We remember that when we left Janusopolis, we still held the spindle of our homeland in our hands.

t_ch: 那时候可天真了，还以为半神就能用纺锤能纺出命运的丝线，将朋友们联系在一起……
t_en: Back then, *we* were so naive — thinking that as demigods, *we* could use the spindle to spin threads of destiny and tie our friends together.

t_ch: 那纺锤后来在战火中丢失了。*我们*的旅途也并不顺利。没有黄金裔愿意响应*我们*的号召，甚至还有坏蛋把我们丢进了牢中监禁……
t_en: But that spindle was lost in war. Our journey wasn't smooth either. Not a single Chrysos Heir was willing to heed our call. Worse still, there were villains who imprisoned us in a cell...

t_ch: 直到刻律德菈找到了*我们*，是她让黄金裔们团结一心，将神谕变作律令。
t_en: Until Cerydra found us. It was she who united the Chrysos Heirs and turned the prophecy into law.

t_ch: 真想也让你认识一下她，只可惜…没有这个机会了……
t_en: I wish I could introduce her to you, but alas... that opportunity is long gone.

t_ch: 想听听缇宁经历的故事。
t_en: I want to hear about Trinnon's experiences.

t_ch: 缇宁她安静又知性，很久以前*我们*一块儿从雅努萨波利斯离开的时候，她抱着一小块石板，想要把自己的知识和理想传达给所有响应号召的黄金裔。
t_en: Trinnon is quiet and intellectual. Long ago, when we left Janusopolis together, she carried a small stone tablet, hoping to convey her knowledge and ideals to all the Chrysos Heirs who answered the call.

t_ch: 她一开始遇见了一群好心的吟游诗人，在他们的保护下行走诸邦。
t_en: At first, she encountered a group of kind-hearted bards who protected her as they traveled across many lands.

t_ch: 可是，凡人的生命脆弱无比，百年之后，她就又变成独自一人，而且比以前更不爱说话了……
t_en: However, mortal lives are fragile, and a hundred years later, she was alone again. By that time, she spoke even less than before...

t_ch: 想听听缇安……
t_en: I want to hear about Trianne...

t_ch: …小灰，不用欲言又止的，*我们*已经没那么难过啦。与缇安的告别仪式都已经结束了，再不将脚尖指向明天，缇安也会伤心、生气的。
t_en: ...Little Gray, there's no need to hesitate. We're no longer that sad. We've already held a farewell ceremony for Trianne. If we don't turn our toes toward the future, Trianne will feel sad — and even angry.

t_ch: 言归正传。缇安是*我们*中最勇敢的一个，最早出发的时候，她手里拿着一把小木剑，哼哧哼哧地想亲自去降伏泰坦呢！
t_en: Back to the topic. Trianne was the bravest among us. When she first set out, she held a small wooden sword, huffing and puffing as she dreamed of taking down a Titan herself!

t_ch: 可惜，结果似乎不大好。她被几名泰坦眷属从拉冬追到埃普斯，那可真是一段惊心动魄的逃亡……
t_en: Sadly, it didn't go so well. She was chased by several Titankins all the way from Ladon to Epos in a thrilling escape...

t_ch: 不过，缇安很骄傲地告诉*我们*，正因为被眷属追赶、不得不反复施放「百界门」的经历，她才能成为*我们*中最娴熟的门匠呢！
t_en: But Trianne proudly told us that it was precisely because of those experiences — being chased by the Kins and repeatedly forced to use "Century Gate" — that she became the most skilled gatekeeper among us!

t_ch: 缇宝老师，我想学「百界门」！
t_en: Master Tribbie, I want to learn how to use the "Century Gate"!

t_ch: 小灰，好有志气！可普通人怎么想都不可能学会「百界门」的啦。
t_en: Ambitious, Little Gray! But it is impossible for ordinary folks to understand how to use the "Century Gate."

t_ch: …不过小灰只是看几眼就能学会欧洛尼斯奇迹，听上去也是一件不可能的事情…难道，小灰真的是天才？
t_en: ...That being said, you took one look at Oronyx's Miracle and learned how to use it. That should've been equally impossible... Could Tribbie be wrong and you are a genius all along, Little Gray?

t_ch: 好，决定了，*我们*来教你使用百界门，来创造奇迹吧！
t_en: Alright, it's decided. We will teach you how to use the Century Gate! Let's create some miracles!

t_ch: 小灰，请你在心底呼唤「万径之门」，感受空间的摇曳，聆听遥远的彼方在你耳边的呢喃……
t_en: Little Gray, please call out the "Gate of Infinity" from the bottom of your heart. Feel the wavering of space and listen to the distant murmurs in your ears...

t_ch: 然后，把你感受到的这股力量，像是撒网一样丢出来！
t_en: Then, cast out the power that you sense, like it's a net!
Open!!!

t_ch: …小灰，吓*我们*一跳。但门径好像没打开诶……
t_en: ...Little Gray, you scared us! And it doesn't seem that the gate is opened...

t_ch: 呼——轰炸空间！
t_en: Ha! Space bomb!

t_ch: 不对不对！小灰用力过猛啦，听上去好像要把空间都给消灭掉啦！
t_en: No, no! That's far too violent, Little Gray! It sounds like you want to completely annihilate space itself!

t_ch: 感受不到，不如界域定锚。
t_en: I cannot feel it. Shall I use a Space Anchor?

t_ch: 那是什么…难道是*我们*不知道的雅努斯神迹吗？
t_en: What is that? Is it some kind of miracle of Janus that we do not know about?

t_ch: 看来，学习施展「百界门」对于小灰来说还是过于勉强了。不过不要难过，小灰很厉害！不需要「百界门」，也一定能成为翁法罗斯的大英雄！
t_en: It seems that learning to use the "Century Gate" is a bit too difficult for you now, Little Gray. But don't be disheartened. You are already amazing! We know that you will become a great hero of Amphoreus even without the power of the "Century Gate"!

t_ch: 关于你们的过去……
t_en: About your past...

t_ch: 嗯…小灰已经和缇宁一起去到往世的雅努萨波利斯，看到过*我们*的过去了吧？
t_en: Little Gray, you've traveled with Trinnon to the past Janusopolis and seen our past, haven't you?

t_ch: 缇宁所看见、听见的事物，*我们*也都清楚地感知到了。那段因漫长岁月而蒙尘的往事，借欧洛尼斯的手擦拭，又再度明亮起来。
t_en: Whatever Trinnon has seen and heard, we also sense. Those dusty memories, buried beneath the weight of time, have been polished by Oronyx and now shine brightly once more.

t_ch: 关于你的母亲……
t_en: About your mother...

t_ch: *我们*的妈妈…有关她的记忆是*我们*最宝贵，可也最容易失去的回忆…因为有她陪伴的日子太美好，以至于*我们*总不敢沉湎其中。
t_en: Our mother... Memories of her are our most precious treasures, yet also the easiest to lose. Those days she spent with us were so beautiful that we hardly dared to immerse ourselves in them.

t_ch: 可太久不回想的记忆，等到真的想重温的时候，才会发现它们已然模糊不清，仅在零星几个夜晚，以梦境的方式送给*我们*一个久别重逢的轻吻……
t_en: But memories left untouched for too long become blurred when one finally tries to recall them. They return only fleetingly on rare nights, like a gentle kiss of reunion in our dreams...

t_ch: 比如说，曾有人问*我们*身上戴着的五瓣花有什么意蕴，*我们*三人使劲想呀想，却始终想不起来。
t_en: For instance, someone once asked us about the five-petal flower we wear. We racked our brains, but no matter how hard we tried, we just couldn't remember its meaning.

t_ch: 托小灰的福，*我们*可算是想到，这种五瓣花，其实就和妈妈紧密相关……
t_en: Thanks to you, Little Gray, we finally realized that this five-petal flower is deeply connected to our mother...

t_ch: 啊，小灰或许还不知道。*我们*的妈妈叫做莫忒丝…这正是我们身上所佩五瓣花的名字。这种花在雅努萨波利斯的传说里，象征着思念、感恩，以及跨越时空的爱。
t_en: Ah, Little Gray, you might not know — our mother's name is Mortis, and this flower we wear is named after her. In the legends of Janusopolis, this flower symbolizes longing, gratitude, and love that transcends time and space.

t_ch: 关于缇里西庇俄丝……
t_en: About Tribios...

t_ch: 「拥有三重生命之人」…岁月的磨蚀下，*我们*都快忘记这原本的名字。
t_en: "The one with three lives"... Time's erosion meant we have almost forgotten this original name.

t_ch: 可是，跨越了万千门径、经历过数次分裂与破碎，*我们*也早已不是最初的缇里西庇俄丝了……
t_en: Yet, after crossing countless gates and enduring splits and fractures, we are no longer the Tribios we once were...

t_ch: 千年以后，如命运所暗中谕示，背负雅努斯神权、行于逐火途中的黄金裔，只有三位。
t_en: A thousand years later, as if fate quietly decreed it, there are only three Chrysos Heirs continuing the Flame-Chase Journey and bearing the divinity of Janus.

t_ch: 她们分别是缇宝、缇宁，和在彼岸静静等候的缇安。
t_en: They are Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne who wait silently on the other shore.

t_ch: 关于后来的雅努萨波利斯……
t_en: About what happened to Janusopolis afterward...

t_ch: 在*我们*离开雅努萨波利斯后，这座城邦的黑暗愈发深重。继任的圣女遭受了较之*我们*更加严峻的控制…比如，现在生活在奥赫玛的「传谕梦母」。
t_en: After we left Janusopolis, the darkness that shrouded the city grew heavier. The Holy Maidens who succeeded us endured even stricter control — such as the current "Oracle Dreammother," who resides in Okhema.

t_ch: *我们*不知道，故乡遭受的苦难究竟是*我们*的错，还是暗中酝酿已久的祸根，也不知道究竟该对故乡抱持什么样的情感……
t_en: We don't know if the suffering of our homeland was our fault or if it was a calamity brewing in secret for a long time. Nor do we know what kind of emotions we should harbor toward our homeland...

t_ch: 只是…在许多年后，听闻雅努萨波利斯最终陷落、沦为如今仄暗的重渊，*我们*还是会忍不住数次故地重游。
t_en: But... many years later, when we heard of Janusopolis' final downfall and how it twisted into the dim abyss it is today, we couldn't help but revisit the place several times.

t_ch: 暂时还是别回想了。
t_en: Let's not dwell on it for now.

t_ch: 嗯，小灰说得对…偶尔回想过去，是为了从珍贵的人那里得到力量。可要是过分沉溺于往事…就看不清前路了。
t_en: Mm, Little Gray, you're right... Looking back on the past every now and then is meant to draw strength from those one holds dear. But if one indulges too much in the past... one will lose sight of the path ahead.

t_ch: 下次再聊，缇宝明天见！
t_en: Let's talk another time. I'll see you tomorrow, Tribbie!

t_ch: 好呀好呀，「明天见」可是个特别、特别好的祝福呢。小灰明天见！
t_en: Okay, sure! "See you tomorrow" is such a wonderful blessing! See you tomorrow, Little Gray!

t_ch: 圣城…大家都很喜欢这里。无论历经多少失却与离别，只要还能看见奥赫玛的昼光，未来就一定明亮。
t_en: The holy city... Everyone loves this place. No matter how much everyone loses or how many farewells everyone endures, as long as humanity can still see Okhema's daylight, the future will always be bright.

t_ch: 嗷呜！你好，我是属于你的奇美拉，请问有什么需要我做的吗？嗷呜——
t_en: Awooo! Hello, I'm your chimera. Is there anything you need me to do? Awooo—

t_ch: 握个手试试？
t_en: How about a handshake?

t_ch: 我是{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}神圣造物嗷呜，不要把我和那些缺乏智慧的生物混为一谈。我也是要面子的嗷呜。
t_en: I am a sacred creation of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}, awoo! Don't lump me in with those unintelligent creatures. I have my pride, awoo!

t_ch: 好吧，你突然开始好奇——要是佩佩生活在这个世界，究竟会归在哪位泰坦麾下？
t_en: Alright then, you suddenly wonder — if Peppy lived in this world, which Titan would he follow?

t_ch: 叫两声给我听听？
t_en: How about you bark a few times for me?

t_ch: 嗷、嗷…嗷嗷……
t_en: Awooo! Howhowhowooooo...

t_ch: 嗷嗷…嗷嗷嗷嗷！
t_en: Awawawaw... ooo... ooo!

t_ch: 小奇美拉用极其清澈的眼神望着你，发出了与平时截然不同的轻柔叫声。
t_en: The little chimera gazes at you with exceptionally clear eyes, letting out soft, tender calls completely different from its usual tone.

t_ch: …怎么连声线都变了。
t_en: ...Even your voice has changed.

t_ch: 别夹了。
t_en: Hey. Stop the fake cute sounds.

t_ch: 嗷呜——累死了。这就是你喜欢的类型吧，满意了嗷呜？
t_en: Awooo— this is exhausting. Is this the type you like? Are you satisfied, awooo?

t_ch: 我都做到这个程度了，以后不准说奇美拉不可爱，嗷呜嗷呜！
t_en: I've gone this far, so from now on, don't you dare say chimeras are not cute! Awooo awooo!

t_ch: 你为什么不去工作？
t_en: Why aren't you working?

t_ch: 你是说生命花园的那些奇美拉吧，它们过很辛苦嗷呜！
t_en: Ah, you mean those chimeras in the Garden of Life? Their lives are so hard, awoo!

t_ch: 听说最近来了个魔鬼监工，成天让它们干活，还要以排行榜的第一名为目标，可你说嗷呜…队伍名次，和奇美拉的生活有什么关系嗷呜？
t_en: I heard there's been a devil of a taskmaster lately, constantly pushing them to work and demanding they aim for first place in the leaderboard. But you tell me, awoo... does the rank have anything to do with the lives of chimeras, awoo?

t_ch: 我不想工作嗷呜，就逃到这来了。你要好好对待我嗷呜！
t_en: I don't want to work, awoo, so I escaped here. You'd better treat me nicely, awoo!

t_ch: 没想到吧，那个人就是我！
t_en: For your information, that taskmaster is me!

t_ch: 我要把你卖到花园去！
t_en: I'm going to sell you to the Garden!

t_ch: …嗷呜？！
t_en: ...Awooo!?

t_ch: 嗷、嗷…嗷嗷…我只是一只可怜又弱小的奇美拉，不要对我抱有任何期待嗷呜！
t_en: Awooo... Awo... awo... I'm just a poor, weak Chimera — don't expect anything from me, awoo!

t_ch: 暂时没什么需求。
t_en: There isn't anything I want for now.

t_ch: 嗷呜——那我就继续躺平了。有事情再叫我嗷呜。
t_en: Awooo — then I'll just rest here. Come back to me if you need anything, awoo.

t_ch: 我想…给你一个拥抱。
t_en: I want... to give you a hug.

t_ch: 遐蝶如今身在冥界，守候着翁法罗斯的生死流转。现在你之所见，只是复现而出的残像。
t_en: Castorice is now in the nether realm, guarding the cycle of life and death for Amphoreus. What you see now is simply a reflection, a lingering shadow of the past.

t_ch: 记忆的残像似乎晃了晃，过往对话的回忆因其承载的情感之重杂糅在一起……
t_en: The fragment of memory seems to waver — memories of words once spoken blend together due to the emotion they contain...

t_ch: 谢谢你，为我实现这个心愿。
t_en: Thank you for fulfilling this wish of mine.

t_ch: 彼岸…十分寒冷，也是人们口中的哀伤之地。
t_en: The shore across... is cold, and is also referred to as the land of sorrows.

t_ch: 但凭借这一抹心跳，和怀抱的温热，冥界仿佛也变作温柔的归处。
t_en: Yet with this heartbeat, and the warmth of that embrace, the nether realm somehow seems to be a gentle sanctuary.

t_ch: 谢谢你送我回到人间。
t_en: Thank you for bringing me back to the mortal world.

t_ch: …我知道，你能做到。
t_en: ...I knew you could do it.

t_ch: 而我也终于能够确信……
t_en: And now, I can finally be certain...

t_ch: 我的触碰，我的拥抱，并非只有冰冷的剥夺……
t_en: My touch and embrace can do more than just bring ice-cold loss...

t_ch: 它同样带着生命的体温。
t_en: They also carry with them the warmth of life.

t_ch: 你…不回来了么？
t_en: You're... not coming back?

t_ch: 嗯…我要留在这里，拥抱自己的命运了。
t_en: I will stay here and embrace my destiny.

t_ch: 冰冷的死荫…将由我来照亮。
t_en: I will illuminate the cold shadow of death.

t_ch: 在冥界，记得给我寄冥信片。
t_en: In the nether realm, don't forget to send me a "nether postcard."

t_ch: 记忆的残像沉默不语，因为这是不曾发生过的对话。
t_en: The shadow of the memory remains silent, for this conversation never truly occurred.

t_ch: 别了，阁下。愿我们…在新世界再会。
t_en: Farewell, then. May we... reunite in the new world.

t_ch: 纷争世以来的漫长岁月，有无数世人、无数眷属、无数黄金裔与我、与奥赫玛、与泰坦们产生了命运的交织。不知你要问哪些人？
t_en: Over the many long years since the Era Bellica, there have been countless people, Titankins, and Chrysos Heirs who have formed bonds of destiny with myself, Okhema, and the Titans. Which ones are you referring to?

t_ch: 留在冥界的遐蝶。
t_en: Castorice, who remains behind in the nether realm.

t_ch: …遐蝶，找到了她在漫长岁月里踽踽独行、一直追寻的答案。
t_en: ...Castorice finally found the answer she's been searching for all these years, walking alone in the long journey.

t_ch: 我会为吾师她们忧虑，曾对万敌心怀忌惮，也总替白厄未来将负的重担而不安。
t_en: I worry for my teacher and the others, I was once fearful of Mydei, and I am always uneasy about the weight of the burden that Phainon will carry in the future.

t_ch: 唯有蝶，向来无需我耗费心力——
t_en: Only Cas has never needed my concern —

t_ch: 我心中有数：当她念出自己追寻已久的答案后，其命运便能迎来圆满。
t_en: I knew that once she finds the answer she's long sought, her fate will finally be fulfilled.

t_ch: 与我十分甚至九分像的白厄。
t_en: My almost-doppelgänger, Phainon.

t_ch: …无缺的英雄。时至今日，我仍会如此评价白厄。作为最完美的黄金裔，他注定在逐火之旅中背负沉重的救世使命。
t_en: ...The perfect hero. To this day, that is still the opinion I have of Phainon. As the most-perfect Chrysos Heir, he is fated to bear the heavy fate of saving the world in the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 托阁下的福，如今逐火之旅将近终点，世人企盼已久的新世界已然在向我们招手。
t_en: Thanks to you, the Flame-Chase Journey is nearly at its end, and the new world the people have long awaited is now reaching out to us.

t_ch: 而属于白厄的命运，也已步步趋近…他将如烈阳一般冉冉升起，接下我的职责，为翁法罗斯的人们照亮前路。
t_en: As for Phainon's destiny, it too approaches step by step... He will rise like a blazing sun, take on my responsibilities, and light the way for the people of Amphoreus.

t_ch: 你们的大表演家，那刻夏。
t_en: The Great Performer among you, Anaxa.

t_ch: 阿那克萨戈拉斯，我与他积怨已久，也原以为这样一位渎神的学者会一直是逐火之旅的阻力。
t_en: I've held a grudge against Anaxagoras for a long time, and I once thought that such a heretical scholar would always be a hindrance on the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 但他的学说，却再度驳斥信仰，以自己的性命掀起了弑神的骇浪……
t_en: But his philosophy has once again refuted faith, and stirred up a torrent capable of slaying gods with his life...

t_ch: 阿那克萨戈拉斯的一生都在追逐着真理，他是个优秀的学士。祝他在新世界能够得偿所愿吧。
t_en: Anaxagoras dedicated his life to seeking the truth. He was an exceptional scholar. I hope that, in the new world, he finds what he was searching for.

t_ch: 笑容很甜的风堇。
t_en: Hyacine, who has a really sweet smile.

t_ch: 上次与你介绍风堇时，我提到她是「昏光庭院」的首席护理师。但事实上，她也是这个组织的继承人与管理者。
t_en: The last time I introduced Hyacine to you, I mentioned she is the chief healer of the Twilight Courtyard. But in truth, she's also the heir and manager of this organization.

t_ch: 「昏光庭院」存世已久，只是因天空一族式微而逐渐凋亡。风堇让这个名号再临世间，以微光与温柔疗愈众人…想必也背负着殷切的梦想吧。
t_en: The Twilight Courtyard has existed for a long time, though it faded into obscurity with the decline of the Sky people. Hyacine has brought this name back into the world, healing others with her gentleness and light... She must be carrying her own deep dreams and aspirations.

t_ch: 见钱眼开的赛飞儿。
t_en: Money-crazed Cipher.

t_ch: 赛法利娅…她曾是黄金裔们亲密的伙伴，也曾与我们朝夕相处、嬉笑怒骂。
t_en: Cifera... She was once a close companion of the Chrysos Heirs, someone we spent our days laughing, arguing, and sharing joys and sorrows with.

t_ch: 可不知从何时起，她突然变得与我们那般疏离，整日在奥赫玛之外漂泊羁旅，连与她面对面道声好都是那么奢侈。
t_en: But somewhere along the way, she became distant and spent all her days wandering beyond Okhema, and it became a rare luxury just to greet her face-to-face.

t_ch: 是「翻飞之币」的火种令她不堪重负，还是厌倦了黄金裔的使命，抑或是…我不知何时犯下的无名罪愆，无可挽回地惹恼了她……
t_en: Was it the Coreflame of the Coin of Whimsy that became too much for her, or did she simply grow weary of the Chrysos Heirs' mission? Or perhaps... it was some nameless fault of mine, some mistake that irreparably angered her...

t_ch: …呼，抱歉，我有些失态了。近来，我的状态总不大好。
t_en: ...Phew, sorry, I lost my composure. I haven't been feeling well lately.

t_ch: 无需替我担心。都已捱过那么久的岁月了，至少，我该能见证逐火之旅结束的那一天……
t_en: Don't worry about me. After all these years, at least I should be able to witness the day the Flame-Chase Journey comes to an end...

t_ch: 都是熟人了，不问了。
t_en: I know everyone well, and there's nothing more I need to know.

t_ch: 我要听旧昏光庭院的历史！
t_en: I'd like to hear more about the history of the old Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 旧昏光庭院的故事…当然可以。正好今天有空，我就和灰宝聊聊吧！
t_en: The story of the old Twilight Courtyard... Of course. I have some free time today, so I'll have a chat with you, Grayie!

t_ch: 很久很久以前，在艾格勒的空中堡垒里，生活着不同部族的空中子民。
t_en: Long ago, in Aquila's sky fortress, there lived Sky denizens from various tribes.

t_ch: 在他们之中，有人尊崇烈阳的灼热，有人喜欢阴雨的清凉，有人偏爱冬雪的壮丽，有人向往微光的柔和。
t_en: Among them, some revered the scorching heat of the sun, some enjoyed the coolness of rain, some marveled at the grandeur of winter snow, while others longed for the soft glow of twilight.

t_ch: 不同部族的天空子民虽然都领受着艾格勒的祝福，彼此间却因信仰不同，总会产生不必要的冲突，直到一位伟大的黄金裔英雄现世……
t_en: Though all the Sky tribes received Aquila's blessings, their differing beliefs led to unnecessary conflicts, until the emergence of a great Chrysos Heir hero...

t_ch: 不知道灰宝有没有听说过我的先祖，「阳雷骑士」塞涅俄丝？
t_en: I wonder if you've heard of my ancestor, Seliose the Daythunder Knight?

t_ch: 在缇宝大人她们传布了救世的神谕后，塞涅俄丝可是第一个响应号召、击败泰坦的黄金裔哦。
t_en: When Lady Tribbie spread the prophecy of salvation, Seliose was the first Chrysos Heir to answer their call and defeated a Titan.

t_ch: 那为何艾格勒的火种没有归还？
t_en: But why wasn't Aquila's Coreflame returned?

t_ch: 据说在艾格勒将被击败的时候，天空也即将与泰坦一同坠落。为了防止世间万物被苍穹碾碎，塞涅俄丝不得不支撑起天空……
t_en: It's said that when Aquila was on the verge of defeat, the sky itself was also about to fall with the Titan. To prevent the world from being crushed by the heavens, Seliose had no choice but to hold up the sky...

t_ch: 凡人弑神，真的假的？
t_en: Mortal slaying gods — could that really be true?

t_ch: 灰宝，先祖塞涅俄丝也是黄金裔哦。可不要忘了，不久之前，你不也和白厄阁下他们一起击败了强大的尼卡多利吗？
t_en: Grayie, my ancestor Seliose was a Chrysos Heir as well. Don't forget that you and the others defeated mighty Nikador together not long ago.

t_ch: 在塞涅俄丝击败艾格勒后，天空的子民们就来到了大地生活。
t_en: After Seliose defeated Aquila, the denizens of the sky descended to live on the earth.

t_ch: 他们被塞涅俄丝挑战苍穹、支撑天空的牺牲壮举所感动，决心弥合曾经种群之间的分歧，构建出大家都能和谐相处的居所。
t_en: They were so moved by Seliose's heroic act of challenging the Titan and holding up the sky that they decided to bridge the divides between their tribes and create a place where all could live in harmony.

t_ch: …这就是天空后裔和昏光庭院的由来啦！
t_en: ...And that's how the descendants of the sky and the Twilight Courtyard came to be!

t_ch: 呀，灰宝！今天阳光特别不错，谢谢刻法勒，你也是来晒太阳的吧。
t_en: Ah, Grayie! What a gorgeous sunny day, thank Kephale. You're here to soak up some sun too, aren't you?

t_ch: 还是说…你是有什么烦心事，想要找人倾诉一下？
t_en: Or perhaps... Is there something on your mind that you want to talk about?

t_ch: 想和你说说话！
t_en: I just want to chat with you!

t_ch: 好呀，我也很乐意和灰宝聊天呢！
t_en: Sure! I'd love to talk with you too, Grayie!

t_ch: 昏光庭院是个什么样的组织？
t_en: What kind of organization is the Twilight Courtyard?

t_ch: 昏光庭院呀…曾经，它是一个弥合阴晴晨昏的疗愈之所，天空后裔和谐共处的族群。
t_en: The Twilight Courtyard... once, it was a healing haven for bridging day and night, where the descendants of the Sky coexisted in harmony.

t_ch: 在漫长的岁月中，战争、黑潮，诸多的灾难与阴霾使它失去了光彩，并最终解离、消逝。
t_en: But over the long years, war, the black tide, and countless disasters and shadows stole its radiance, and eventually, it dissolved and faded away.

t_ch: 而在翁法罗斯坠入永夜之后…我在树庭重新建立起了昏光庭院。
t_en: After Amphoreus fell into eternal night... I rebuilt the Twilight Courtyard in the Grove.

t_ch: 我本想重建那疗愈人心的庭院，但或许是我太爱操心，总要给每位来访的朋友解决身体上的问题。因为我希望当他们离开时，从心灵到身体都能焕然一新。
t_en: I wanted to recreate that heart-healing courtyard, but maybe I'm just too much of a worrywart — I can't help but try to address everyone's physical ailments whenever they visit. I hope that they're renewed both in body and spirit when they leave.

t_ch: 结果…大家就把昏光庭院当作是医疗机构，把我叫做首席护理师啦。
t_en: And so... everyone started treating the Twilight Courtyard like a clinic and calling me the chief healer!

t_ch: 大家都喜欢你和你的庭院。
t_en: Everyone really likes you and your courtyard.

t_ch: 对一名志在抹去患者伤痛的医师来说，最让人开心的事情莫过于此啦。
t_en: For a healer determined to erase their patients' pain, nothing makes me happier than hearing that!

t_ch: 还想听旧昏光庭院的历史。
t_en: I'd like to hear more about the history of the old Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 啊，旧昏光庭院的故事…那可就太长、太长啦。下次有机会再和灰宝讲吧！
t_en: Ah, the story of the old Twilight Courtyard... that would be a very long tale. Way too long, in fact. Let's save it for another time, okay, Grayie?

t_ch: 风堇有什么喜欢做的事吗？
t_en: What do you enjoy doing, Hyacine?

t_ch: 嗯…我觉得平时的日常生活，就是足够令我喜欢的事情了。
t_en: Hmm... I think my normal, everyday life is what I really enjoy.

t_ch: 请你闭上眼睛想象——在林叶簌簌、凉风习习的庭院中，到处是闪闪发亮的饰品和软乎乎的抱枕，餐桌上摆着可口的蜜饯和暖融融的茶水。
t_en: Close your eyes and imagine this — a courtyard with rustling leaves and a cool breeze. Shimmering ornaments and fluffy cushions are scattered everywhere, while the dining table is laid with delicious sugary preserves and warm, soothing tea.

t_ch: 一束微光从头顶的天井滑下来，它并不耀眼，却足够填满整个庭院。
t_en: A soft, glimmering ray of light streams in from the skylight above. It's not blinding, but just bright enough to fill the entire courtyard.

t_ch: 而你与你的朋友们，就在这样一个童话般的场景里畅聊，聆听彼此的烦恼，纾解身心的疲敝……
t_en: And there you are, with your friends, in this fairytale scene, chatting freely, sharing your worries, and easing the fatigue plaguing each other's minds and souls...

t_ch: 感觉身体暖暖的！
t_en: I feel all warm and fuzzy!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，也有人觉得这样的场景能带来扫去烦躁的清凉感哦。
t_en: Hehe, and others might say such a scene would give them a cool and refreshing feeling, one to wash away their worries.

t_ch: 温暖而不炎热，凉爽而不寒冷…疗愈心灵的秘境，本就应该能让不同的人感受到相近的安心啊。
t_en: Warm, but not stifling. Cool, but not chilly... A sanctuary of healing should bring everyone the same sense of comfort, even if they themselves are different.

t_ch: 想象不到，像个梦境。
t_en: Just can't picture that. It feels like a dream.

t_ch: 是吗？其实，初次听闻昏光庭院的许多人，也都觉得这只是个美好的梦。
t_en: Really? Actually, many who first hear about the Twilight Courtyard think it's just a lovely dream, too.

t_ch: 不过，这样也不坏。因为等大家真的看到了庭院，就会有种美梦成真的惊喜感了！
t_en: But that's not a bad thing. Because when they finally see the courtyard for themselves, they're in for a wonderful surprise — it's like their dream has come true!

t_ch: 最近我在奥赫玛到处转悠，寻找合适的角落，我想在奥赫玛重新建造一处昏光庭院。
t_en: Recently, I've been wandering around Okhema, looking for the perfect spot to rebuild the Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 到时候，灰宝一定要常来坐坐呀！
t_en: When it's ready, Grayie, be sure to visit often, okay?

t_ch: 翁法罗斯的医术是什么样的？
t_en: What is Amphorean healing like?

t_ch: 咦，灰宝居然也对医术感兴趣吗？看来你果然很适合加入我们呢！
t_en: Oh? Grayie, you're actually interested in the art of medicine too? Seems like you really are a great fit for us!

t_ch: 在遥远的黄金世，人人都拥有没有尽头的漫长生命。直到塞纳托斯降临，疾病、痛苦和死亡才开始降临大地。
t_en: In the ancient Era Chrysea, everyone had endless, immortal lifespans. It wasn't until Thanatos descended that illness, suffering, and death arrived.

t_ch: 畏惧苦痛、厌恶苦痛并渴望战胜苦痛的人们成为了这方世界的第一批医师。他们从零开始，摸索着治愈创伤的办法。
t_en: Those who feared pain, detested it, and sought to overcome it became the first healers in our world. They started from nothing, slowly learning how to treat injuries and ailments.

t_ch: 经过千年的积累，行走大地的医师们总结出了许多可靠的知识。如果要用最简洁的方式和灰宝阐述的话，那就是：轻者用药调养，重者手术治疗，危者施加祝福。
t_en: Over millennia, the healers wandering the land accumulated a wealth of reliable knowledge. If I were to summarize it for you, Grayie, it'd be something like: For minor ailments, we use medicine to nurse them back to health. For serious injuries, we perform surgeries. And for those in critical condition, we invoke blessings.

t_ch: 我知道灰宝来自遥远的天外，在其他的世界，人们又是用什么样的方法治愈伤患的呢？
t_en: But I know you come from a faraway land beyond the sky, Grayie. How do people in other worlds heal the sick and wounded?

t_ch: 用葫芦。
t_en: With gourds.

t_ch: 葫芦…是那种腰细肚鼓的中空果实吗？莲食学派的植物著论里好像有提到，不过在现在的翁法罗斯已经很少见了。
t_en: Gourds... You mean those hollow fruits with slim waists and rounded bellies? I think they're mentioned in the botanical treatises by the Lotophagists, though they're quite rare in the Amphoreus of today.

t_ch: 原来葫芦还能治病吗…要是树庭还保留着这类植物的种子就好了。
t_en: So gourds can even be used for healing? If only the Grove still had seeds from plants like that...

t_ch: 用令旗。
t_en: With banners.

t_ch: 令旗…是什么？
t_en: Banners? ...What are those?

t_ch: 三角形的小旗子，挥一挥就可祛除人们的病痛…好方便的道具，要是我也能有这样的一面神奇的旗子就好啦。
t_en: Small triangular flags that can cure ailments just by being waved around... What a convenient tool! I wish I had a miraculous flag like that too.

t_ch: 用棺材。
t_en: With coffins.

t_ch: 棺材？！是我想的那个，死者长眠所处的大匣子吗？
t_en: Coffins!? You mean the large boxes where the deceased are laid to rest?

t_ch: 唔…实在想象不到，棺材该怎么用于治疗……
t_en: Hmm... I really can't imagine how coffins could be used to cure people...

t_ch: 下次再聊吧！
t_en: Let's catch up another time!

t_ch: 好呀，欢迎灰宝再来找我聊天！
t_en: Sure! I'd be happy to chat with you again, Grayie!

t_ch: 下次再聊吧！
t_en: Let's catch up another time!

t_ch: 好呀，欢迎灰宝再来找我聊天！
t_en: Sure! I'd be happy to chat with you again, Grayie!

t_ch: 你好，搭档！这段日子辛苦了…你果然在翁法罗斯逐笔写下了属于自己的英雄史诗。未来的逐火征程里，还请继续与我们并肩作战！
t_en: Hey there, partner! Must've been tough for you lately... Sure enough, you've been writing your own epic in Amphoreus. Let's keep on chasing that flame together, you and me!

t_ch: 你专程来找我，是有什么要紧事吗？我随时可以帮忙！
t_en: Did you come looking for me because there's something urgent you need help with? I'm ready anytime!

t_ch: 想找你聊聊天。
t_en: I want to chat with you.

t_ch: 当然可以！你想聊些什么？
t_en: Of course! Anything you'd like to talk about?

t_ch: 你有什么爱好？
t_en: Have any hobbies?

t_ch: 我喜欢做的事情还挺多。但论得上爱好的…恐怕也就只有战斗和鉴宝了。
t_en: There are quite a lot of things I enjoy. But as for what I'd call a hobby... I'm afraid only fighting and treasure appraisal make the cut.

t_ch: 战斗，我也喜欢！
t_en: Fighting? Now we're talking!

t_ch: 对吧？我喜欢挥剑时那种心无旁骛、只为将对手击倒的感觉。
t_en: Right? I love that feeling of being completely focused while swinging a sword, thinking of nothing but defeating the opponent before you.

t_ch: 但是万敌却说我并非真正热爱战斗……
t_en: But Mydei says I don't truly love battle...

t_ch: 「唯有生死一线悬的瞬间，你才能从救世的职责中短暂脱身。」这是他的原话。
t_en: "It's because you can only briefly free yourself from the duty of saving the world in those fleeting moments at the threshold of death." That's what he said.

t_ch: 哈…我哪有那么高尚啊。比起我，如今独自在悬锋城的万敌，背负的职责才更沉重吧？
t_en: Ha... I'm not that noble. Compared to me, Mydei carries far heavier duties alone in Castrum Kremnos, don't you think?

t_ch: 鉴宝？好出戏的爱好。
t_en: Treasure appraisal? Really didn't expect that one from you.

t_ch: 可别小看了我，我和西塔罗斯先生学了很久的鉴宝技术，现在已经是小有所成。
t_en: Don't sell me short like that — I studied treasure appraisal with Mr. Theodoros for a long time, and I've made some good progress. I'm on my way to being an expert.

t_ch: 我为数不多的日常零用，除了武器的维护，基本都花在这个副业上了。
t_en: Aside from maintaining my weapons, most of my modest daily expenses go toward it.

t_ch: 倒不是有多喜欢收藏古玩…练习鉴宝，总需要批量收购那些似真似假的货品。只是，或许是我运气不佳，往往买下一麻袋，满满的全是赝品……
t_en: It's not that I'm particularly fond of collecting antiques... Practicing appraisal requires buying lots of items that might or might not be genuine. But, perhaps due to my bad luck, I often end up with a whole sack full of fakes...

t_ch: 预言中的黄金裔有许多个吗？
t_en: Are there many Chrysos Heirs in the prophecy?

t_ch: 有的搭档，有的。预言中的黄金裔当然不止一个了，一共有十二位，都是能够归还火种、承袭泰坦神权的英雄。
t_en: Yes, partner, there are. The prophecy speaks of way more than one — twelve Chrysos Heirs in total, each a hero capable of returning the Coreflames and inheriting the divinity of the Titans.

t_ch: 但只有你被称为救世主。
t_en: But you're the only one called the Deliverer.

t_ch: 哈哈…初次听到这样的称呼的时候，我还有些害臊呢，毕竟老大不小了，早就不是当初那个举着木剑、口口声声要拯救世界的稚子了。
t_en: Haha... When I first heard that title, I felt a bit embarrassed. After all, I'm not exactly a kid anymore, the kind who'd wave a wooden sword and prattle on about how they're going to save the world.

t_ch: 不可否认，直到加入逐火之旅，我依然觉得自己不够成熟，尤其是在与诸位伙伴相较之时……
t_en: To be honest, even after joining the Flame-Chase Journey, I still felt pretty immature — especially when compared to my companions.

t_ch: 我没有阿格莱雅的思虑与手腕，没有万敌的信念与心性，没有遐蝶的理智与审慎…而与缇宝老师她们比起来，我还少了一份乐观与执着。
t_en: I don't have Aglaea's foresight and strategy, Mydei's conviction and resilience, or Castorice's rationality and caution... I even lack Tribbie's optimism and determination.

t_ch: 可是，我却被伙伴们称作最完美的黄金裔，被奥赫玛的人民奉为救世主。
t_en: Yet, my companions call me the most perfect Chrysos Heir, and the people of Okhema revere me as the Deliverer.

t_ch: 鼓励、希冀，还有渴求与祈祷…我的肩上，背负了太多不可辜负的东西。
t_en: Encouragement, hope, yearning, and prayers... The heavy burdens my shoulders bear are ones that I cannot possibly let down.

t_ch: 所以，我也不能妄自菲薄。救世主的名号…坦然相受，又有何妨？
t_en: So, I've come to terms with it. If they call me the Deliverer... why not gracefully accept the title?

t_ch: 但现在好像不足十二个。
t_en: But there don't seem to be twelve of you right now.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅曾说…逐火是不断失却的旅途。早在千年之前，同样有一批伟大的黄金裔，歃血为盟、共同踏上击落泰坦的征程。
t_en: Aglaea once said... the Flame-Chase is a journey of constant loss. A thousand years ago, another group of great Chrysos Heirs forged a blood pact and embarked on a journey to strike down the Titans.

t_ch: 也许是命运使然…第一次逐火之旅虽成功归还了数枚火种，却在血与泪中遗憾告败。
t_en: Perhaps it was fate at work... Though they succeeded in returning several Coreflames during the first Flame-Chase Journey, it ended in blood, tears, and failure.

t_ch: 那批英雄前辈，以生命铸成伟业，为人们积攒了信心、指引了前路。
t_en: Those heroic predecessors laid the foundation for greatness with their lives, gave the people confidence, and pointed the way forward.

t_ch: 虽不见其人，但每次前往创世涡心、聆听泰坦之声时，总能感觉到前辈们的英灵仍与我们相伴而行。
t_en: Though we can't see them, every time we head to the Vortex of Genesis or listen to the voices of the Titans, I can feel the spirits of our predecessors alongside us.

t_ch: 关于盗火行者和你的过去……
t_en: About the Flame Reaver and your past...

t_ch: 哈…我看上去有那么脆弱吗？好像每位与我相处了一段时间的战友都会觉得我有段沉重到难以放下的过去。
t_en: Haha... do I look that fragile? It seems that every companion I've spent time with assumes I have a past so heavy it's hard to let go of.

t_ch: 其实还好啦！在见到盗火行者以前，我或许还会被那段阴影所紧缚，毕竟在童年的我看来，那道高大的黑色身影是那样残忍而不可战胜。
t_en: Honestly, it's not that bad! Before I encountered the Flame Reaver, I might have still been bound by that shadow. After all, as a child, I saw that tall, black figure as cruel and invincible.

t_ch: 但是…当与它面对面对峙、剑锋相交之时，那段深埋回忆中的悲惧，便会燃为奔腾的战意与怒火。
t_en: But when I stood face-to-face with them, crossing blades, those buried fears and sorrow ignited into a surging battle spirit and anger.

t_ch: 它不只是你的敌人。
t_en: They are not just your enemy.

t_ch: 没错。如今，盗火行者站在逐火之旅的对立面，它是奥赫玛的敌人，是所有对「再创世」抱有美好期待的人们的敌人。
t_en: Exactly. Now, the Flame Reaver stands in opposition to the Flame-Chase Journey. They are the enemy of Okhema and all who hold beautiful hopes for the Era Nova.

t_ch: 或许你会认为，如今我有了「公报私仇」的机会。
t_en: You might think I've now gained the chance for personal revenge.

t_ch: 但请你相信我…我的私仇，在翁法罗斯的未来面前，根本微不足道。
t_en: But trust me... my grudge pales in comparison to Amphoreus's future.

t_ch: 它已经被我们打败了。
t_en: They've already been defeated by us.

t_ch: 没错。集合众英雄的力量，我们在悬锋城将他合力击败了。看万敌那一击的威力，他就算侥幸没有彻底陨灭，也一定已经元气大伤。
t_en: That's right. With the combined strength of the heroes, we defeated it in Castrum Kremnos. With Mydei's final strike alone, even if it managed to avoid complete annihilation, it must be gravely weakened.

t_ch: 不过，我们绝不能掉以轻心。在他卷土重来以前，我一定会想办法磨砺剑锋，保护伙伴们…至少，决不让缇安老师那样的悲剧在我眼前再度发生。
t_en: Still, we can't let our guard down. Before it returns, I'll sharpen my blade to protect my companions... At the very least, I won't let tragedies like what happened to Trianne happen before my eyes again.

t_ch: 没什么了，下次再聊！
t_en: That's all for now. Let's chat next time!

t_ch: 没问题，我随时奉陪！
t_en: Sure. Feel free to find me any time!

t_ch: 没啥要紧事，下次再聊！
t_en: Nothing urgent for now. Let's chat next time!

t_ch: 没问题，我随时奉陪！
t_en: Sure. Feel free to find me any time!

t_ch: 你坐下，群星般的光芒在你面前荡漾，水波璀璨，空气中仿佛有阵阵回响。
t_en: As you sit down, the light of the stars begins to ripple before you, the waves shine, and the air seems to resonate with echoes.

t_ch: 缇宝…没想到她还有那样的过去。
t_en: Tribbie... I didn't expect her to have such a past.

t_ch: 身为雅努萨波利斯的圣女，她曾经受万人敬仰，却因偷窃火种而饱受敌意。那些曾发誓保护她的卫士，将矛头对准了她。
t_en: As the Holy Maiden of Janusopolis, she was once revered by thousands, yet she suffered great hostility for stealing the Coreflame. Those guards who once swore to protect her turned their spears against her.

t_ch: 为了传递神谕，她将身躯化整为零、分散为万千个体，以至自我的边界都逐渐模糊，心智退化为孩童……
t_en: To deliver the prophecy, she shattered her body into countless pieces, scattering herself into myriad forms, until even the boundaries of her sense of self blurred, and her mind regressed to that of a child...

t_ch: 如今就连缇安，仅存的三者之一，也耗尽了力量。今后，门匠的职责将由缇宝继续履行。她已下定了决心，*她们*已下定了决心。
t_en: Now, even Trianne, one of the last three, has exhausted her strength. From now on, the duty of the gatekeeper will be carried on by Tribbie. She has made her decision — they have made their decision.

t_ch: 逐火…果真是不断失却的旅途。
t_en: Losses... are indeed a constant on the Flame-Chase journey.

t_ch: 你坐下，群星般的光芒在你面前荡漾，水波璀璨，空气中仿佛有阵阵回响。
t_en: As you sit down, the light of the stars begins to ripple before you, the waves shine, and the air seems to resonate with echoes.

t_ch: 万敌…真令人刮目相看。
t_en: Mydei... is truly impressive.

t_ch: 你想起那个狮子般的男人。据说狮子的一生会经历许多次迁徙，最后在距离出生地很遥远的地方落脚……
t_en: You recall the leonine man. It's said that a lion's life is marked by many migrations, eventually settling far from where it was born...

t_ch: 迈德漠斯，悬锋的新王，他曾一度在奥赫玛收获同伴和认可。但为了世界的明天，他选择离开，投身于永无止境的战争。
t_en: Mydeimos, the new king of Kremnos, once gained companions and recognition in Okhema. Yet, for the sake of the world's future, he chose to leave and devote himself to endless war.

t_ch: 或许正是因为这份荣耀与担当，归乡的游子终结了悬锋王朝千年以来沐血代冠的传统。命运果真被他战胜了。
t_en: Perhaps it was precisely this honor and responsibility that allowed the homecoming wanderer to end the thousand-year Kremnoan tradition of being crowned in blood. Fate was indeed conquered by him.

t_ch: 那就祝他常战常胜吧。
t_en: Then, I wish he achieves victory in every fight.

t_ch: 你坐下，群星般的光芒在你面前荡漾，水波璀璨，空气中仿佛有阵阵回响。
t_en: As you sit down, the light of the stars begins to ripple before you, the waves shine, and the air seems to resonate with echoes.

t_ch: 袭击树庭的怪人…到底是何方神圣？
t_en: The strange entity who attacked the Grove... who could they be?

t_ch: 你想起那浑身黑衣的蒙面剑士，他不算强大，但比你见过的任何敌人都要诡异。寻常战斗中足以致胜的手段，在他面前却三番五次地失效……
t_en: You recall the masked swordmaster clad in black. It wasn't particularly powerful, but it was more bizarre than any enemy you've faced. Techniques that would normally secure victory failed repeatedly against it...

t_ch: 难道他是黑潮的化身？又或是某位泰坦的孑遗？若非万敌及时继承了「纷争」的神力，那牛皮藓似的家伙怕是要再追杀你们一阵。
t_en: Could it be an embodiment of the black tide? Or perhaps the remnant of some Titan? If not for Mydei inheriting the divinity of "Strife" in time, that persistent foe might have kept pursuing you.

t_ch: 如今，它已然落败。但就像「钟表小子」系列动画中的鳄鱼老板那样，反派很难真正被杀死——它们总是会从不知哪个下水道里重新爬出来。
t_en: Now, it's been defeated. But like the Crocodile Boss in the "Clockie" series of cartoons, villains are hard to truly kill — they always seem to crawl back out of some unknown sewer.

t_ch: 应该不会太棘手…大风大浪都挺过来了。
t_en: It shouldn't be too troublesome... we've weathered far greater storms.

t_ch: 你是…啊，我在公民大会上见过你，你和阿格莱雅大人、白厄大人并肩而立，我记得你的名字…叫{NICKNAME}对不对？
t_en: You're... Wait, I've seen you before! At the citizens' assembly, standing with Lady Aglaea and Lord Phainon. Your name is {NICKNAME}, isn't it?

t_ch: 没错，看来我已经很有名了。
t_en: That's right. Seems I've made quite a name for myself.

t_ch: 哈哈，有名归有名。可不全是好名声呀。
t_en: Hah, you certainly have. Though not all of it is good.

t_ch: 错的，请叫我银河球棒侠。
t_en: Wrong. Call me the Galactic Baseballer.

t_ch: 哈哈，做好事、做坏事都不留名，是吧？
t_en: Hah! So you do good deeds or bad ones, all while staying incognito?

t_ch: 你看错了，我不认识黄金裔。
t_en: You've got the wrong person. I don't know any Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 不不不，我可没有看错，毕竟我也在现场嘛。
t_en: Oh, no, I didn't mistake you. I was there. I saw it myself.

t_ch: 我是隶属于元老院的记录员，据我所知，外乡人{NICKNAME}曾在奥赫玛城中擅自打开宝箱取走财物，打碎飞天坛递，还让黄金短矛在闹市穿梭……
t_en: I'm an archiver for the Council of Elders. According to our records, the outworlder {NICKNAME} once opened a treasure chest in Okhema without permission, shattered the Flying Amphora, and let the Golden Short Spear fly through a bustling market...

t_ch: 我错了，请不要让我赔偿。
t_en: I'm sorry! Please don't make me pay for damages!

t_ch: …我没有要赔偿。
t_en: ...I'm not asking for reparations.

t_ch: 这些事情，我都没有做过。
t_en: I didn't do any of those things.

t_ch: …可你都是堂堂正正当着大家的面做的呀。
t_en: ...But you did. And in full view of everyone.

t_ch: 记录员，你应该记些正事。
t_en: Archiver, maybe you should focus on more serious matters.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，这确实就是正事。
t_en: Honestly, these are serious matters.

t_ch: 您误会了，我并非是向你索赔，我也不是警告…等等，你是不是因为我在给元老院做事，就暗自将我当成坏人了？
t_en: Look, I'm not seeking reparations, and I'm not here to threaten you either. Wait, do you think I'm some kind of bad guy just because I work for the Council of Elders?

t_ch: 元老院，不都是坏人吗？
t_en: The Council of Elders... Aren't they all bad guys?

t_ch: 当然不是啊！咱单位一部分人的立场，不代表我本人的立场。
t_en: Of course not! A handful of people don't represent the entire Council — or me, for that matter.

t_ch: 拿我问罪的人都是坏人！
t_en: Anyone accusing me is a bad guy!

t_ch: 那您的善恶标准还挺松弛的。
t_en: Your standards for good and bad seem... flexible.

t_ch: 首先，我先声明，我不是你理解的「坏人」。我只是树庭结业不久的学生，给元老院做事纯粹是为了丰厚薪酬，绝无任何政治立场。
t_en: For the record, I'm not who you think I am. I'm just a new graduate from the Grove, working for the Council of Elders purely for the generous pay. I have zero political stance.

t_ch: 我刚才提到的事，虽然都确实记录在案，但我跟那些老顽固们不一样，这些事我都打算睁一只眼闭一只眼，没打算找你问责，只是想确认一下你就是那个人罢了——
t_en: All those things I mentioned are in the records, yes, but unlike the old fossils in the Council, I don't plan to hold you accountable. I just wanted to confirm that you're the person in those records...

t_ch: 我想着你和我一样都是年轻人，你可能像我一样对政治没那么感兴趣…你不知道，这里尽是满嘴政治的老人，我都快闷死了。也许我们能聊得来……
t_en: Because, honestly, I think you're like me: Young and uninterested in politics. You wouldn't believe how suffocating it is, being surrounded by people who talk about nothing else. I thought maybe we could relate...

t_ch: 怎么样，救救黎明云崖的年轻人，和我聊一会吧？
t_en: How about it? Save a fellow youth from the Dawncloud and chat with me for a while?

t_ch: 「我们站在光历的转折点上！」哎，这句话我自己都不太信……
t_en: "We stand at the turning point of the Light Calendar!" Ugh, even I don't believe that nonsense...

t_ch: 这里的人说话都好无聊啊，我上辈子做了什么要惩罚我一个年轻人来这种地方上班…幸好有你{NICKNAME}，能陪我随便聊会天吗？
t_en: The people here are so dull. What did I do in a past life to deserve this punishment — working in this place with nothing but old men for company? At least there's you, {NICKNAME}. Would you keep me company for a bit and just... talk?

t_ch: 来吧，随便聊聊就行。
t_en: Come on. Just a small talk.

t_ch: 当然，还得靠您和我降低这里的平均年龄。聊点什么呢？
t_en: Of course, it's up to us to bring down the average age around here. So, what should we talk about?

t_ch: 你说你是刚从树庭结业的学生？
t_en: You mentioned you just graduated from the Grove?

t_ch: 没错，之前我是那刻夏老师的学生。不知道您是否了解那刻夏老师，作为老师他人是挺好的，可就是说…他的课程没法转化为专业技能……
t_en: That's right. I studied under Professor Anaxa. Not sure if you've heard of him — he's a good person and a decent teacher, but... his courses don't exactly translate into practical skills...

t_ch: 经常有这种「不实用」的专业…
t_en: Sounds like one of those "impractical" fields...

t_ch: 都怪我选专业的时候光顾着看哪个老师长得帅了。
t_en: Well, it's my fault for choosing a major just because the professor was good-looking.

t_ch: 我明明听说那刻夏很注重实践。
t_en: I heard Anaxa emphasizes hands-on practice, though.

t_ch: 实践是有实践，但没有什么工作需要这种实践。
t_en: Oh, there's plenty of practice — it's just not the kind of practice that translates into any real occupational skills.

t_ch: 所以我认清了现实，早早结业，领受父母之命出来找工作了。元老院的工作就是家父推荐来的——也因此，我躲过了树庭惨案。
t_en: So, I came to terms with reality, graduated early, and followed my parents' advice to start job hunting. My father recommended this position at Council of Elders — which, incidentally, helped me avoid the Grove tragedy.

t_ch: 尽管我不算个好学生，但我很喜欢那刻夏老师…如果不用考虑我那糟糕的成绩，我还是想继续待在树庭里钻研学业。
t_en: Even though I wasn't the best student, I really liked Professor Anaxa. If it weren't for my poor grades, I'd have loved to stay in the Grove and focus on my studies.

t_ch: 听上去，你不想在这继续工作？
t_en: Sounds like you're not too keen on staying in this job.

t_ch: 怎么说呢…我每天都有无数次离职的冲动，但是它们终究只是冲动，不会付诸实践。毕竟日子还得过，我需要这份工作。
t_en: Well... I think about quitting at least a dozen times a day, but it's just that — thoughts, not actions. Life has to go on, and I need this job.

t_ch: 它薪酬待遇好，工作本身也不复杂，主要是和各种各样的权贵沟通——事实上，我精通于此，我比大部分老前辈做得更好。
t_en: The pay is great, the benefits are excellent, and the work itself isn't complicated. Most of it involves liaising with various elites — and honestly, I'm better at it than most of the old-timers here.

t_ch: 真正让我抱怨的是，我预感十年后，二十年后、甚至三十年后，我的工作内容都会一成不变，而我迟早也会变成我讨厌的老顽固。
t_en: What really gets to me is the thought that ten, twenty, or even thirty years down the line, I'll still be doing the exact same thing. And eventually, I'll become one of those stodgy old bureaucrats I can't stand.

t_ch: 每当我和家里抱怨这些的时候，他们就会说「你看看外面都怎样了，你有这么一份工作就很了不起了」…他们说的没错，我会继续干下去。
t_en: Every time I complain about this to my family, they just say, "Look at the world out there — having a job like yours is already remarkable." They're not wrong. So, I'll stick with it.

t_ch: 工作是工作，生活是生活…
t_en: Keep your personal life separate from your work...

t_ch: 还是你懂我，我会把它们分得很清楚的。
t_en: Exactly! You get me — I keep the two separate.

t_ch: 肯定有很多人羡慕你的工作。
t_en: There must be plenty of people who envy your position.

t_ch: 肯定会有的吧，人们总是对不了解的工作有所向往。
t_en: Probably. People always admire what they don't really understand.

t_ch: …工作内容我就不奢求有所改变了，只求能多来点年轻同事。
t_en: ...I've given up hoping for changes in my job, but I do wish for more young colleagues around here.

t_ch: 有没有什么元老院的黑幕？
t_en: Any scandals of the Council of Elders you can share?

t_ch: 黑幕可还行…我不知道什么黑幕。不过嘛，我这里有一些非常不靠谱的八卦，没人知道那是真是假，我给你讲讲。
t_en: Scandals? Hah, I wouldn't know anything about that. But I do have some wildly unreliable gossip. No one knows if it's true or not — want to hear it?

t_ch: 我还想活着，别告诉我。
t_en: Let's skip it. I'd like to stay alive.

t_ch: 你放心，没人相信那是真的。
t_en: Don't worry. No one actually believes it.

t_ch: 细说，我竖起耳朵听！
t_en: All right, spill it — I'm all ears.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我就知道你感兴趣。
t_en: Ah, I knew you'd be interested.

t_ch: 在我刚来的时候，有个元老，名字我就不方便说了，年事已高，元老院指派了一名活泼开朗的年轻人去照料他的起居。
t_en: When I first joined, there was a senior senator — I'll skip his name — who was quite elderly. The Council assigned a bright, cheerful young person to look after his daily needs.

t_ch: 我本来以为我能认识下年轻的同事，但是吧，我的上司让我别去打招呼，什么也别问，我这人也听话，就真的什么也没问。
t_en: I thought it'd be a chance to get to know a fellow junior colleague, but my superior told me not to approach them, not to ask anything, and to just mind my own business. Being the obedient type, I did exactly that.

t_ch: 一段时间以后，那位元老逝世了。再过了一段时间，听说元老们又递补了新成员——问题就出在这里！
t_en: A while later, the senator passed away. Not long after that, they appointed a new member. Here's where it gets weird!

t_ch: 那位新成员看着和当时照料元老的年轻人长得一模一样，可就是神情不是很像，她不再活泼开朗了，反倒一副老态龙钟的样子。
t_en: This new member looked exactly like the young caretaker, except their demeanor had completely changed. They weren't cheerful anymore — just weary and aged beyond their years.

t_ch: 这就是我那非常不靠谱的八卦了，你记在心里便好，不要告诉别人。
t_en: And that's my utterly unreliable bit of gossip. Keep it to yourself, though. Don't go spreading it around.

t_ch: 没事，我先走了。
t_en: Well, I'm heading off, then.

t_ch: 元老院的前辈们说「我们站在光历的转折点上」，或许真的如此。再见，希望我们都能顺利越过转折点。
t_en: The senior senators love saying, "We stand at the turning point of the Light Calendar." Maybe they're right. Goodbye. Hope we both make it through this turning point.

t_ch: 没事，我先走了。
t_en: Well, I'm heading off, then.

t_ch: 元老院的前辈们说「我们站在光历的转折点上」，或许真的如此。再见，希望我们都能顺利越过转折点。
t_en: The senior senators love saying, "We stand at the turning point of the Light Calendar." Maybe they're right. Goodbye. Hope we both make it through this turning point.

t_ch: 尤瑞托斯，<unbreak>30</unbreak>万利衡币…埃姬娜，<unbreak>750</unbreak>万衡币…保卢斯，<unbreak>200</unbreak>万利衡币……
t_en: Eurytos, <unbreak>300,000</unbreak> Balance Coins... Aegina, <unbreak>7.5</unbreak> million Balance Coins... Paulus, 2 million Balance Coins...

t_ch: 那么{NICKNAME}呢？
t_en: So, how about {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}？也欠我钱吗？你这家伙别打扰我核对账单啊……
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}? Do you owe me money too? Hey, don't distract me while I'm checking my accounts!

t_ch: 这是勒索名单吗？
t_en: Is this some kind of extortion list?

t_ch: 勒索？开什么玩笑，瞧好了，这可是账单——上面的人都欠我钱！
t_en: Extortion? Don't be ridiculous. Take a good look — this is an accounting ledger. Everyone on it owes me money!

t_ch: 你…先别说话，我记得你，阿格莱雅身边的贵客，对不对？我可没有记错人吧。
t_en: You... Wait a minute, I know you. You're Aglaea's esteemed guest, aren't you? I'm not mistaken, am I?

t_ch: 我对你印象还不错，所以{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，以后要是缺钱了，可以随时来找我帮忙——鉴于你的贵客身份，我可以不要利息。
t_en: I've got a good impression of you, so if you ever find yourself short on cash, {F#young lady}{M#young man}, feel free to come to me for help. Given your status, I won't even charge interest.

t_ch: 你是做慈善的？
t_en: Are you running a charity now?

t_ch: 呵呵，并非慈善。不是所有人都能有这样的待遇。
t_en: Charity? Far from it. Not everyone gets this kind of privilege.

t_ch: 不会是诈骗吧…
t_en: Sounds like a scam...

t_ch: 对陌生人警惕点没什么坏处，不过我是诚心提供帮助。
t_en: There's nothing wrong with being cautious around strangers, but I assure you, I'm offering genuine help.

t_ch: 我知道你有些顾忌，这么和你说吧——我是个「暴发户」，从下面上来的那种。
t_en: Let me put it this way — I'm what people refer to as "nouveau riche," someone who made it big from the ground up.

t_ch: 这黎明云崖的老顽固们瞧不起我，我也瞧不起他们，但是我总得想办法在这里站稳脚跟，你说对吧？我就特别愿意和你这样年轻有为的人交流。
t_en: The old geezers on Dawncloud look down on me, and I don't think much of them either, but I still have to establish myself here. You get what I mean? That's why I enjoy talking to young, capable people like you.

t_ch: 换句话说，我将对你的帮助视为一种投资，我相信我的眼光。哈哈，这样吧，先给你一些「小心意」就当是交个朋友了。
t_en: Think of my help as an investment — I trust my instincts. Haha, here, consider this little gesture a token of friendship.

t_ch: 怎么样，打算接着聊聊吗？
t_en: What do you say? Want to keep chatting?

t_ch: 嗯？{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，我们算是认识了，有什么想聊聊的吗？
t_en: Hmm? {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, now that we know each other, is there anything you'd like to talk about?

t_ch: 随便聊聊可以吗？
t_en: Can we just chat about anything?

t_ch: 行啊，你想知道什么就随便问吧。
t_en: Of course. Ask me anything you want.

t_ch: 你是做什么生意的？
t_en: What kind of business are you in?

t_ch: 哦，这个啊…也没什么可隐瞒的——我是靠奥赫玛之外的黑潮发财的。
t_en: Oh, that? It's no secret — I made my fortune outside Okhema, in the black tide.

t_ch: 逃难的人需要大地兽搬运行李，我会提供大地兽；有人需要食物和取暖，我会提供营帐和食物…我提供各式各样的帮助，只不过它们并不是无偿的。
t_en: When refugees needed dromases to haul their belongings, I provided dromases. When they required food and warmth, I offered tents and supplies. I provide all kinds of aid — just not for free.

t_ch: 很多人瞧不上这个，觉得我不地道——但恕我直言，如果没有我，谁能帮上他们？如果我没有收钱，谁能持续帮助他们？
t_en: A lot of people look down on this kind of work, calling it unscrupulous. But let me be blunt — without me, who else could help them? And without charging, who could sustain this kind of assistance?

t_ch: 当然了，会有很多人指责我的。他们希望我只有救人的善心，而放弃发财的野心——这黎明云崖里有太多这样站着说话不腰疼的家伙了。
t_en: Of course, I get plenty of criticism. People want me to save lives with a pure heart, free of ambition. This place — Dawncloud — is full of self-righteous types who sit comfortably while judging others.

t_ch: 所以，我想到了一个解决方法——只要这些占据着道德制高点的人能给我钱的话，我会提供救援，我可以不收逃难者一分一毫。
t_en: So, I came up with a solution: If those moralistic types want me to provide aid for free, they can pay for it themselves. I'll waive all fees for the refugees.

t_ch: 你猜他们会掏钱吗？
t_en: Care to guess how many of them actually step up to fund it?

t_ch: 你说老顽固瞧不起你？
t_en: You mentioned the old-timers look down on you?

t_ch: 哈哈，这你就有所不知了——有些个长老啊、贵族啊，之所以能坐在他们现在的位置上，不过是仰赖祖辈积德，含着金奶嘴生下来的。
t_en: Haha, let me tell you something — some of these elders and aristocrats only hold their positions because of their ancestors' luck. They were born with a silver spoon in their mouths.

t_ch: 但如今这世道…那些外邦的就不说了——不远万里逃难过来，能带多少家当？就连本地也有不少贵族，只剩下一点表面体面。
t_en: These days, though... Let's not even talk about the foreigners who fled here with barely anything to their name. Even among the local aristocrats, many are just keeping up appearances.

t_ch: 我手上有份账单，上头全是向我借钱的蠢货——本事没有，架子倒是不小…瞧不起我，但还是要向我这种「暴发户」赔笑脸。
t_en: I've got a ledger full of these fools who borrowed money from me. They may look down on me, but they still come groveling to this "nouveau riche" when they need cash.

t_ch: 可惜，我也对他们有所求，我也会借给他们。他们瞧不起我，我也瞧不起他们，事实如此，不影响我们之间的利益关系。
t_en: Sure, I lend it to them — because I also need something from them. They despise me, and I despise them. It doesn't change the fact that we're tied together by mutual interests.

t_ch: 你讨厌贵族还借他们钱？
t_en: You despise the aristocrats, yet you still lend them money?

t_ch: 这么说吧，有些钱，我明知道他们还不上，但还是会借，你知道为什么吗？
t_en: Let me put it this way — some money I know they won't be able to pay back, but I still lend it to them. Do you know why?

t_ch: 就因为他们说话「有分量」，而我可以用上。
t_en: Because their words actually make a difference, and that's what I want.

t_ch: 在我看来，奥赫玛的管理者，无论是元老院、还是黄金裔，都不过徒有其名——每年税金浪费了不少，效率远不如我们这些商人。
t_en: To me, Okhema's administration — whether it's the Council of Elders or the Chrysos Heirs — is just an empty shell. Every year, so much tax money is wasted with little to show for it. The bureaucrats can't hold a candle to us merchants when it comes to efficiency.

t_ch: 奥赫玛想要进步，就需要更先进、更实际的社会架构与管理方式。我有心成为这个伟大变革的推动者，奈何我只是个「平民」。
t_en: If Okhema wants progress, it needs a better, more practical system of governance. I'd love to spearhead that change, but alas, I'm just a "civilian."

t_ch: 他们有权，而我有钱。我该怎么做已经不言而喻了吧！
t_en: They have power, and I have money. Surely, you can see where this is going.

t_ch: 不，我不懂。
t_en: Nope, still not following.

t_ch: 啧…等我改变奥赫玛的行政效率时，你就会知道的。
t_en: Tch... Well, you'll understand when I've streamlined Okhema's governance.

t_ch: 公司欠你个offer…
t_en: You deserve an offer... from a certain Corporation.

t_ch: …公司…什么公司？我自己就是老板。
t_en: ...Corporation? What corporation? I'm my own boss.

t_ch: 我的理想是独立于奥赫玛管理者之外，拆解官僚机构，削减多余监管法规和浪费的开支，让陈腐的管理者早点滚出黎明云崖。
t_en: My ambition is to create an administration independent of Okhema's rulers — one that dismantles the bureaucracy, slashes excessive regulations, and cuts wasteful spending. It's time to send those antiquated officials packing from Dawncloud.

t_ch: 先不聊了，我还有事。
t_en: Let's talk later. I've got things to do.

t_ch: 有缘再见啦，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，正好我手头还有些账目没处理完……
t_en: Until next time, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. Speaking of which, I've got some ledgers waiting for me...

t_ch: 先不聊了，我还有事。
t_en: Let's talk later. I've got things to do.

t_ch: 有缘再见啦，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，正好我手头还有些账目没处理完……
t_en: Until next time, {F#young lady}{M#young man}. Speaking of which, I've got some ledgers waiting for me...

t_ch: 啊，这位贵客，请留步——您不是奥赫玛土生土长的住民，对吧？
t_en: Ah, esteemed guest, please wait a moment — you're not a native of Okhema, are you?

t_ch: …像我这样的外乡人还挺多的？
t_en: ...Are there many outworlders like me here?

t_ch: 其实我也是外乡人。
t_en: Actually, I am an outworlder, too.

t_ch: 我从小看着负世泰坦长大的！
t_en: I grew up watching the Worldbearing Titan!

t_ch: 哈哈，您真会开玩笑……
t_en: Haha, you've got a sense of humor...

t_ch: 是这样，我呢，名叫忒斯庇斯·米里奥多卢，是一名来自米里奥斯的剧作家。
t_en: Well, let me introduce myself. I'm Thespis Mylonopoulos, a playwright from Milios.

t_ch: 我离开自己家乡的时间已经太长了，很多关于家乡的印象已经开始模糊，和奥赫玛渐渐重合在了一起…我需要有一处将它们区分开的「地标」——
t_en: I've been away from my homeland for so long that my memories of it have started to blur, merging with my impressions of Okhema. I need a "landmark" to keep them distinct.

t_ch: 你看，那巨大的{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}奥体，它沉默不语地俯视着我们，如此的惊诧感，只有在黎明云崖才会感受得尤其深刻。
t_en: Look at that colossal {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, who gazes down at us silently, and its imposing presence is uniquely profound here on Dawncloud.

t_ch: …故乡覆灭时，我正在奥赫玛巡演，无家可归的我，自那以后就定居在了奥赫玛。现在想来，我生活于此的时光早已超过了故乡「米里奥斯」。
t_en: ...When my homeland fell, I was touring Okhema. Left without a home, I've lived here ever since. Now, I've spent more years in Okhema than I ever did in Milios.

t_ch: …不好意思，您的家乡是怎样的呢？
t_en: ...I'm sorry, but what was your homeland like?

t_ch: 我已经回想不起来了。
t_en: I can no longer recall.

t_ch: 看来您和我有着相同的烦恼。
t_en: Seems like we share a similar predicament.

t_ch: 那是辆星间旅行的列车！
t_en: That was an Express traveling between the stars!

t_ch: 我没听懂，就当您是将群星当作了故乡吧。
t_en: I'm not sure I understand, but I'll take it that you see the stars as your homeland.

t_ch: 能听见那绵延百里的汽笛。
t_en: The sound of a whistle stretching for miles...

t_ch: …听上去那是您离开家乡时的声音。
t_en: ...That must be the sound you heard when you left your homeland.

t_ch: 我经常向奥赫玛城中的外乡人打听他们的故乡，在不同人口中，故乡虽然有不同的描述，却有着相近的「韵脚」——也许所有人的故乡都共享一处。
t_en: I often ask the outworlders here in Okhema about their homelands. Though their descriptions vary, there's a common "melody" to them — as if all homelands share a single origin.

t_ch: 那是一座看不见的城市，但她是我们灵魂的的永恒归宿…我有生之年最大的愿望，就是以自己的家乡为原型，创造一部永世流传的伟大戏剧。
t_en: It's an invisible city, one that serves as the eternal resting place for our souls. My greatest wish in life is to create a timeless play inspired by my homeland.

t_ch: 抱歉，占用了您不少时间，请收下这份小小礼物。当然，如果您喜欢戏剧，我们可以再深入聊聊……
t_en: Sorry to take up so much of your time. Please accept this small token. Of course, if you're a fan of theater, we can talk more...

t_ch: 人生一世，能真正接触并熟悉的人不过数百…但戏剧提供了一扇窗口，能让我们近距离凝视一些平常不会触碰的人性角落。
t_en: Life is short, and the people we truly know and understand might number only in the hundreds. But theater opens a window, letting us gaze closely into corners of humanity we'd otherwise never touch.

t_ch: 故乡，那座看不见的城市，正在我的思绪中逐渐成型。想必与您交谈一番，我还会有所收获吧，怎么样，打算再聊聊吗？
t_en: My homeland — that unseen city — is gradually taking shape in my mind. I believe that by talking with you, I'll find even more inspiration. So, what do you say? Shall we continue this conversation?

t_ch: 想和你随便聊聊。
t_en: I'd like to chat with you.

t_ch: 当然，我喜欢「随便聊聊」。关于故乡，关于戏剧，关于你我的当下，让我想到哪说到哪。
t_en: Of course, I enjoy casual conversations. Whether it's about homeland, theater, or the present moment, let's see where our thoughts take us.

t_ch: 你在创作以故乡为原型的戏剧？
t_en: You're writing a play inspired by your homeland?

t_ch: 没错。不过她既是故乡，也并非故乡——她是千万人共同的故乡。
t_en: Yes. But it's both my homeland and not my homeland — it's a homeland shared by countless others.

t_ch: 请您想象一座云端之上的城市，它的结构像干燥的浴球：人们在城市何处生活，那里就会「吸水」膨胀；而这被比作「水分」的介质，正是市民们的欲望和记忆。
t_en: Imagine a city floating above the clouds, structured like a dried bath sponge. Wherever people live, that part of the city "absorbs water" and swells. The "water" represents the desires and memories of its citizens.

t_ch: 基于此，本质上完全不同的许多城市，都以同样的名字出现在云端之上…她既是信徒们的圣城，也是弄潮儿的时尚之都，更是学者的书院、勇者的兵营……
t_en: Because of this, many fundamentally different cities appear in the clouds under the same name. It's the holy city for believers, the fashion capital for trendsetters, a library for scholars, a barracks for warriors...

t_ch: 由于这个城市的名字有着如此之多的含义，渐渐地，人们反而不再使用那个名字，而只用「城市」称呼自己的家乡。
t_en: Over time, the name of the city gained so many meanings that people stopped using it altogether. Instead, they simply called it "The City."

t_ch: 只是，在那座城市里有一件奇怪的事——从邻居到过客，几乎每个人都在为这片土地上自己看不见的部分感到不安。
t_en: However, there's something strange about this city — everyone, from neighbors to passersby, feels uneasy about the parts of the city they cannot see.

t_ch: 比如说…信徒担心学者的研究让城市神秘不再，学者担心勇士的冒险为城市树立了过多敌人；勇士又讨厌弄潮儿的奢华，弄潮儿厌恶信徒的不思进取…诸如此类。
t_en: For example, believers fear that scholars' research will strip the city of its mystery. Scholars worry that warriors' adventures bring too many enemies to the city. Warriors resent trendsetters for their decadence, while trendsetters despise believers for their complacency.

t_ch: 这些市民对一切都充满抱怨，大家的唯一共识，是只有{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}会公平地对待他们。
t_en: These citizens complain about everything, yet they all agree on one thing: Only {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}, treats them all fairly.

t_ch: 好——作为一出悲剧的舞台，那座城市大致来说就是这样。
t_en: Well... That's what the city looks like as a stage for my tragic play.

t_ch: 坏了，我没有搞懂！
t_en: Wait... I don't get it!

t_ch: 我向来追求雅俗共赏…你的意见，我会考虑进去的。
t_en: I strive to create works that appeal to both the refined and the common audience. Your feedback will certainly help me improve.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我完全理解了。
t_en: I completely understand.

t_ch: 呵呵，太好了，我起初还有点担心观众会不理解呢。
t_en: Wonderful! I was a bit worried that the audience might not understand at first.

t_ch: 我打算用「米里奥斯」来命名这个城市——纪念一下我那在黑潮中毁灭的故乡！
t_en: I've decided to name this city "Milios" — a tribute to my homeland, destroyed in the black tide.

t_ch: 要不聊聊你的过去的经历？
t_en: Would you mind sharing your experiences?

t_ch: 我的过去啊…要从何说起呢？正如你所知道的，我是米里奥斯人，不是奥赫玛人——不过我定居奥赫玛的起因，可能和大部分外地人不太一样。
t_en: My experiences, huh... Where should I begin? As you can see, I'm a native of Milios, not Okhema. But the reason I settled here might be quite different from most outsiders.

t_ch: 算起来那也是很多年前了…那时候我自觉年少成名，和志同道合的几个演员朋友一起，凑了点路费就打算开启一场环游整个世界的巡回演出。
t_en: It happened many years ago... Back then, I thought I was a rising star. A few actor friends and I, driven by shared ambition, scraped together enough money to embark on a grand tour around the world.

t_ch: 奥赫玛是我们的第四站，我们在这里的表演终于收获了大家的欢迎，也打算在这里多停留一段时间，多积累一些经验和财富。
t_en: Okhema was our fourth stop. It was here that our performances finally received genuine applause. Encouraged, we decided to stay a little longer, hoping to gain more experience and earn a bit of wealth.

t_ch: 其实最为主要原因是，钱兜已经见底，我们不得不留下挣些返乡路费。
t_en: Though honestly, the main reason was that we were broke. We had no choice but to stay and make enough money to afford our journey home.

t_ch: 当时我们不可能想到，这个决定救了我们所有人一命——就在第二个月，米里奥斯被黑潮毁灭，据称无人生还。
t_en: Little did we know, that decision saved our lives. Just two months later, Milios was consumed by the black tide. They say no one survived.

t_ch: 从此故乡，就成了我永远的精神故乡了。呵呵。
t_en: And so, my homeland became my eternal spiritual sanctuary. Haha.

t_ch: 我对戏剧不熟，有什么理念吗？
t_en: I'm not very familiar with theater. Do you have any particular creative philosophy?

t_ch: 理念吗…虽说我的确写过不少剧本，但可能并没有什么能称之为「理念」的东西。
t_en: Philosophy? Hmm... I've written plenty of plays, but I wouldn't say I have anything grand enough to be called a "philosophy."

t_ch: 「创作要付出真情实感？」那好像是小孩子们识字课本上就有的要求。
t_en: "Write with genuine emotions?" That's something kids learn from their first reading lessons.

t_ch: 「作品要服务社会现实？」实话说，如果不这么做，创作者根本就没法赚钱。
t_en: "Serve societal reality with your work?" Honestly, if you don't, you can't make a living in this field.

t_ch: 至于什么「悲剧比喜剧深刻」，或者「喜剧比悲剧有建设性」…我并不在意这种争论，也愿意尝试不同的情节安排。
t_en: As for debates like "tragedy is deeper than comedy" or "comedy is more constructive than tragedy"... I don't really care. I'm open to trying different story structures.

t_ch: 如果说真的有什么故事是我拒绝创作的——那大概只有「无论如何都无法让我获得感动」这一种情形吧。
t_en: If there's one kind of story I absolutely refuse to write, it's the kind that fails to move me — no matter what I do.

t_ch: 人们常说，深刻理解角色的人生、拥有和角色同样充沛的感情是「演员的自我修养」…对剧本创作者来说，当然更必须如此。
t_en: People often say that deeply understanding a character's life and emotions is an actor's preparation. For a playwright, this is even more essential.

t_ch: 你搞戏剧真的能赚到钱吗？
t_en: Can you really make money in theater?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈！确实，赚钱是一切的基础，没人会记得赔钱的戏剧。
t_en: Haha! That's a good one. Yes, money is the foundation of everything. Nobody remembers a play that leads to bankruptcy.

t_ch: 不过，就像最高端的食材往往只需用最简单的调料加以烹饪…如果说到靠创作赚钱，那么最重要的也不过是「让自己成为观众」。
t_en: But, just like the finest ingredients only need the simplest seasoning, when it comes to making money through art, the most crucial thing is "to become your own audience."

t_ch: 自己喜欢的就能赚钱？
t_en: You mean if you like it, it'll sell?

t_ch: …可以这么说。
t_en: ...You could put it that way.

t_ch: 自己给自己的剧大量买票？
t_en: You buy out tickets to your own shows?

t_ch: 哈哈，到底是谁会挣钱呢？
t_en: Haha! Who can truly make a living off their art?

t_ch: 市场和创作本身，有时候甚至会产生结构性的矛盾。举个例子吧…比如有个剧作家，曾经以创作爱情剧出名。
t_en: Sometimes, there's an inherent conflict between the market and the act of creation itself. Let me give you an example. Imagine a playwright known for romantic dramas.

t_ch: 但是在这个黑潮泛滥的时代，已经有很多观众不再欢迎爱情剧；面对这样的市场挑战，这个剧作家就必须真正理解观众为什么「不再欢迎爱情剧」。
t_en: In this age consumed by the black tide, many audiences have lost interest in love stories. For this playwright, the real challenge isn't just writing — they must understand why people have turned away from romance.

t_ch: 如果不想清楚这一点…那么即使这位剧作家，在纯艺术的意义上创作出了伟大的作品…恐怕也只会叫好不叫座吧。
t_en: If they can't figure that out... even if they produce a masterpiece in the purest artistic sense, it'll likely go unappreciated, or worse, unnoticed.

t_ch: 一切艺术作品都不过是时代的涟漪。而致力于从时代逃脱的创作者——所谓「真正的」艺术家，很难用艺术去养活自己。
t_en: Every work of art is just a ripple in the tide of its time. Those who strive to escape their era — the so-called "true artists" — often find it hard to sustain themselves through their creations.

t_ch: 不打扰你思考创意了。
t_en: Well, I'll leave you to your creation.

t_ch: 哈哈，谈不上什么打扰，请慢走。
t_en: Haha, feel free to come back if you want to talk. Take care.

t_ch: 不打扰你思考创意了。
t_en: Well, I'll leave you to your creation.

t_ch: 哈哈，谈不上什么打扰，请慢走。
t_en: Haha, feel free to come back if you want to talk. Take care.

t_ch: …又来一位，饶了我吧。我的魅力简直就是{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}刻在我身上的罪恶。来吧，把你的石板交给我吧。
t_en: ...Another one? Come on. My charm must be a curse engraved upon me by {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}. All right, hand over your teleslate.

t_ch: 石板？什么石板…
t_en: Teleslate? What teleslate...

t_ch: 你不是要来签名的吗？
t_en: You're not here for an autograph?

t_ch: 啊？不是，我还没说什么…
t_en: Uh? No, I didn't even say anything...

t_ch: 你不用说，我明白的。
t_en: No need to explain, I get it.

t_ch: 不用解释，我知道你也是我的崇拜者。唉，每个人都解释一遍，我已经倦了……
t_en: I know you're one of my admirers. Ugh, having to explain this over and over is so tiresome...

t_ch: 现在请给我签名吧。
t_en: Please give me the autograph now.

t_ch: 那就把石板给我吧。
t_en: Now, give me your teleslate.

t_ch: 谁想要你的签名啊？
t_en: I'm not here for your autograph.

t_ch: 哦？你想引起我的注意吗？这招没用的。
t_en: Oh? Trying to catch my attention with this act? Sorry, but it won't work....

t_ch: …啊，难道你其实没有石板？难道你…其实没想要我的签名？难道我…自作多情？
t_en: Wait, don't tell me you don't even have a teleslate? You... you didn't actually come for my autograph? Was I... imagining things?

t_ch: …你可以签在我的衣服上。
t_en: ...You can sign my clothes.

t_ch: …你现在才发现吗？
t_en: ...You realized just now?

t_ch: 我还以为…啊，不好意思，是我失态了。不过，我很有魅力也是事实嘛。
t_en: I thought... Ah, pardon me, I got carried away. But it's true that I'm very charismatic.

t_ch: 来，仔细看、往这看！瞧瞧这身材，再瞧瞧这肌肉！怎么样，喜不喜欢、羡不羡慕？
t_en: Come and look closely! Right here! Behold, this body, and these muscles! Do you like them? Do you admire them?

t_ch: 「奥凯纳斯运动会」你知道吧？就是那个在奥凯纳斯每四年举行一次、无数城邦竞相参与的著名运动会——我蝉联过三届掷铁饼冠军！
t_en: Surely you know the Okeanas Games? The famous, quadrennial sports event that takes place in Okeanas, with countless city-states participating — I was the three-peat champion of the discus throw event!

t_ch: 我就是掷铁饼的世界第一人——怎么可能有人拒绝得了我的魅力嘛！
t_en: That makes me the world's top discus thrower! How can anyone resist my oozing charm?

t_ch: 好了，言归正传，既然你说不是来要签名石板的，那你找我是想要做什么？
t_en: Anyway, back to what we were saying... Since you aren't here for an autographed teleslate, what do you need from me, then?

t_ch: 你放心，我不会瞎误会了。你来找我，单纯是为了聊天对吧？
t_en: Don't worry, I understand now. You're just here for a simple chat, right?

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Care to chat?

t_ch: 没问题，你可以多问问我的辉煌战绩，我可以给你讲一整天。
t_en: Of course. Ask me about my glorious achievements. I can talk about them all day.

t_ch: 其实我还是不认识你…
t_en: Actually... I still don't know who you are.

t_ch: …哦，你确实可能没听说过我，但是你没听说过我不可能。
t_en: ...Oh, maybe you haven't heard about me, but it's impossible that you haven't heard of me.

t_ch: 本人柯库翁，来自奥凯纳斯，一座以竞技体育闻名于翁法罗斯的古老城邦。
t_en: I am Glykon of Okeanas. It is an ancient Amphorean city-state known for competitive sports.

t_ch: 你可能听说过奥凯纳斯人喜欢追求肉体极限，但是你可能没有见识过极限——现在你就见到了，我就是极限。
t_en: You may have heard that the people of Okeanas love to push the limits of their bodies, but you've probably never seen "the limit." Well, now you have. I am the limit.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，你不会觉得我是在自吹自擂吧？可惜这奥赫玛还没有举办过同等级的大型体育竞技赛事，你不认识我，我不怪你。
t_en: Hahaha, you don't think I'm tooting my own horn, do you? It's a shame that Okhema has yet to host any large-scale sports competition on the same level, so I don't blame you for not knowing me.

t_ch: 现在你认识我了，用不了多久，你会崇拜我。哈哈哈。
t_en: But now that you do, you'll be worshiping me in no time. Bwahaha!

t_ch: 你说的那个运动会…
t_en: About the sports event you mentioned...

t_ch: 是「奥凯纳斯运动会」——整个翁法罗斯最著名的运动会！天啊，真的需要我给你解释一遍吗？这翁法罗斯干脆毁灭算了。
t_en: The Okeanas Games — the most renowned sports event in the entire Amphoreus! Goodness, do I really need to explain it to you again? Amphoreus really is doomed.

t_ch: 「奥凯纳斯运动会」涵盖了你能想到的各种体育竞技，其中包括：田径、拳击、摔跤、角斗、赛马等几十种。
t_en: The Okeanas Games cover every kind of sports competition you can think of, including track and field, boxing, wrestling, gladiatorial combat, horse racing, and so on.

t_ch: 但不同于悬锋人的残忍好斗，运动会讲究的是纯粹的竞技精神——所以请不要将我们和那些不识字的种族归为一谈。
t_en: Unlike the vicious and aggressive ways of the Kremnoans, we promote the immaculate spirit of competition in the Games — so please don't mix us up with those uncouth tribes.

t_ch: 你知道吗？这其中最引人瞩目的就是掷铁饼！而我，是三届掷铁饼冠军！
t_en: And you know what? Discus throw is the most eye-catching event of all! And yours truly is the three-peat discus throw champion!

t_ch: 你这是绝对的统治力啊！
t_en: Absolute dominion!

t_ch: 这就是黎明云崖将我奉为上宾的原因。
t_en: That is why I am held as an esteemed guest at Dawncloud.

t_ch: 真是最引人注目的项目？
t_en: Is it really the most eye-catching event?

t_ch: {F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，没人比我更懂「奥凯纳斯运动会」了。
t_en: {F#Young lady}{M#Young man}, no one understands the Okeanas Games better than I do.

t_ch: 我很怀念桂冠和掌声，圣城也得办奥赫玛运动会才行啊！
t_en: I miss the laurels and applause very much. The holy city needs its own Okhema Games!

t_ch: 有人说过你有些…自恋吗？
t_en: Has anyone ever called you... narcissistic?

t_ch: 哈哈哈——你可以不必那么委婉。我非常自恋！自恋到令人讨厌。
t_en: Bahaha! You can be frank about it. Yes, I am very narcissistic! So much that it annoys people.

t_ch: 但没办法，太有魅力就是会自恋啊！这也是我的烦恼。
t_en: But what can I do? My overflowing charm rubs off on me too! Such is my burden.

t_ch: 作为一名奥凯纳斯人，我的自恋是责任的一部分——通过展示我健美的肉体，让那些不懂竞技精神的人感受其魅力，这便是奥凯纳斯精神。
t_en: As someone from Okeanas, this narcissism is a part of my duty. The Okeanas spirit is to display my fit body and capture those who don't understand the spirit of competition with its excellence.

t_ch: 我听过不少人劝我不要这么张扬，可是这碍着他们什么事了吗？完全没有，他们只是在嫉妒我，我没有义务听命于这些缺乏魅力的家伙。
t_en: Many have advised me to turn it down a notch, but I've never gotten in their faces about it, not at all! They are just jealous, and I am not obligated to take advice from charmless folk.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，怎么样，现在你想要我的签名石板了吗？
t_en: Hahaha, what do you think? Do you want my autographed slate now?

t_ch: 先不聊了，祝你好运。
t_en: I gotta run. Good luck with everything.

t_ch: 哦，好，我经常在附近闲逛，要是想见我了可以随时过来。
t_en: Oh, alright. I usually hang out nearby. Feel free to come over any time you want to see me.

t_ch: 先不聊了，祝你好运。
t_en: I gotta run. Good luck with everything.

t_ch: 哦，好，我经常在附近闲逛，要是想见我了可以随时过来。
t_en: Oh, alright. I usually hang out nearby. Feel free to come over any time you want to see me.

t_ch: 你记得遐蝶曾介绍过「亡语蝶」的来历——有人认为它是死者的最后一道执念，也有人认为它是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的信使。
t_en: You remember Castorice once spoke of the origins of the Eulogistic Butterflies. Some believe them to be the final obsession of the deceased, others believe them to be {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos'{RUBY_E#}s messengers.

t_ch: 死难者众多的地方，总有它们翩飞的身影。
t_en: They are always found in places where many have met their demise.

t_ch: 迷途的人啊，如果还差一句话你就要离开人世，你会说什么？
t_en: Lost human, if you can say one last thing before leaving this world, what would it be?

t_ch: 「睡了，睡得比较死。」
t_en: "I'm going to do a deep eepy."

t_ch: 言简意赅。
t_en: Short and to the point.

t_ch: 「我还想再活五百年…」
t_en: "I want to live five hundred more years..."

t_ch: 需求明确。
t_en: A demand proclaimed.

t_ch: 「再见，崩坏：星穹铁道！」
t_en: "Farewell, Honkai: Star Rail!"

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Farewell.

t_ch: 「我留下开拓的遗产就在…」
t_en: "I left my trailblazing legacy at..."

t_ch: 留下了悬念。
t_en: Such suspense.

t_ch: （不说是不是就不会死？）
t_en: (I live if I stay quiet, right?)

t_ch: 死亡对所有人都是公平的。
t_en: Death is fair to all.

t_ch: 我还记得一些逝者的*临终遗言*，如果你也感到好奇的话……
t_en: I still recall the last words of some of the deceased, if you're curious...

t_ch: *临终遗言*，出自最接近死亡之人。生者与逝者的身份，就在这只言片语中交汇——像是那*临终遗言*带着死亡的分量，让微妙平衡的生命天平最终倒向一侧。
t_en: Last words are from those closest to death. In this fleeting phrase, the identities of the living and the deceased converge — as if the weight of these words would tip the delicate balance of the scale of life irrevocably toward one side.

t_ch: 我还记得一些逝者的*临终遗言*，如果你也感到好奇的话……
t_en: I still recall the last words of some of the deceased, if you're curious...

t_ch: 我对*临终遗言*很感兴趣。
t_en: I'm interested in some last words.

t_ch: 当然，那么你对谁的遗言更感兴趣呢？
t_en: Of course. Whose parting words would you like to hear the most?

t_ch: 讲讲不同城邦的主人…
t_en: The rulers of the different city-states...

t_ch: 我记得曾有位豁达的君主说过「尘事已了，我死而无憾。」
t_en: I recall that an optimistic ruler once said, "I have finished my business here, and I die without regrets."

t_ch: 临终前，有君主被问到谁会是继任者，他只说了「最强者」。
t_en: Someone asked another ruler, on his deathbed, who his successor would be. He only said "The strongest."

t_ch: 还有君主饱受痛苦，她命令着医生说「杀了我，否则你就是弑君者。」
t_en: Another one suffered so much pain that she gave this order to her doctor, "Kill me, or be guilty of regicide."

t_ch: …我记得，还有一位君王临终前只说了一句「水」。
t_en: ...I remember one of the rulers only said "Water" before dying.

t_ch: 讲讲立下战功的英雄…
t_en: The heroes in war...

t_ch: 在敌国被杀的战士，曾在处刑前说「我唯一的遗言，就是我只有一次生命可以献给我的城邦。」
t_en: Before his execution at the hands of the enemy, a soldier said, "The only thing I wish to say in this last moment is that I can only give my life for my city-state once."

t_ch: 更多的战士无法得知死亡何时而至，他说「告诉他们，我身体的一侧疼得厉害」。
t_en: Many more soldiers didn't know when death would come. One said, "Tell them that it hurts really bad on one side of my body."

t_ch: 纵横海域的老船长最后在岸上死于疾病，她说「请帮我穿上衣服，扣上盔甲，让我能够像一名战士一样死去。」她最终被葬在海边。
t_en: The old captain who traversed many seas died from illness on dry land. She said, "Please help me put on my clothes and armor, and let me die like a proper soldier." She was buried by the sea in the end.

t_ch: 讲讲撰写诗篇的诗人…
t_en: The poets who wrote great works...

t_ch: 诗人父亲在临终前询问自己的诗人儿子，「告诉我，亚历山大，凭着你的灵魂和良心，你相信我死后所有的作品仍将流传下去吗？」
t_en: Before his death, a poet asked his poet son, "Tell me, Alexander, on your soul and conscience — do you believe my works will be passed on after I die?"

t_ch: 一位诗人毕生追求着新的诗歌，直至最后——「我不要庭院的医者宣告我的死亡，我要自己宣告这自由。」
t_en: A poet spent her life pursuing new verses, till the very end. "I seek no healer in the Courtyard to declare my demise, for I shall proclaim this freedom with my own cries."

t_ch: 还有一位诗人在演出的过程中死去，他最后一句唱诗是「{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}许诺了我更好的世界。」
t_en: Another poet died during his performance. His final line was, "{RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} promised me a better world."

t_ch: 讲讲寂寂无名的平民…
t_en: The nameless commoners...

t_ch: 有位树庭的哲学家想要伸手摘杏子，却被妻子责备，他说「我就是摘了，又*翁法罗斯粗口*会怎么样呢？」片刻之后，他死了。
t_en: There was a philosopher of the Grove who tried to pick some apricots but was scolded by his wife. He said, "So what if I picked them? What the *Amphoreus profanity* is going to happen?" Moments later, he died.

t_ch: 在被问到感受如何时，有人如此说到，「很好，很舒服。现在我必须睡觉了…把枕头拿走吧——我再也不需要它们了。」
t_en: When asked how they are feeling, someone replied, "I feel good and comfortable. I must sleep now... Take the pillows away. I don't need them anymore."

t_ch: 也有人曾突然感到一阵头疼，随即她的*临终遗言*是「怎么回事？」
t_en: There was a person who felt a sudden headache, and her last words were, "What's happening?"

t_ch: 感谢你，就聊到这里吧。
t_en: Thank you. That's all for now.

t_ch: 你可以随时回来找我。
t_en: You can come back any time.

t_ch: 你似乎很了解*临终遗言*？
t_en: You seem to know a lot about last words.

t_ch: 我并不了解它们，我只是对此感到好奇罢了。
t_en: I don't understand them. I'm merely curious about them.

t_ch: 奥赫玛城曾有诗人在生前说，遗言是「在一息尚存之时，用充满睿智的话语华丽地将自己送达{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的身边。」
t_en: A poet from Okhema once said that last words are "words of wisdom, uttered with a lingering breath, that deliver oneself gracefully to {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos'{RUBY_E#}s side."

t_ch: 然而他自己却发现「人在弥留之际，油尽灯枯，身体和大脑都变得不可靠了。」最后他的遗言仅有一句「如果我们再相逢…」便猝然结束。
t_en: However, he himself discovered that "at the moment before death, one's body and mind become unreliable as the candle burns out." In the end, his last words ended abruptly with, "If we meet again..."
